# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

This repository publishes documentation and configuration for the Rails docs
site. Security fixes are typically applied to the active long-lived branches.

| Branch | Supported |
| --- | --- |
| `main` | Yes |
| `staging` | Best effort |
| `develop` | Best effort |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Please do not open a public GitHub issue for suspected security vulnerabilities.

Instead, report vulnerabilities privately to `security@railsinfra.com` with:

- A clear description of the issue
- Steps to reproduce it
- Any affected routes, pages, or configuration
- The potential impact
- Any suggested remediation, if you have one

You can expect an initial acknowledgement within 5 business days. After that,
we will investigate, determine severity, and coordinate a fix and disclosure
plan with you where appropriate.

## Scope

This policy covers vulnerabilities in this repository, including:

- Documentation site configuration
- Client-side code and components in this repo
- Deployment or environment handling documented here

If your report is primarily about a third-party service or dependency, we may
coordinate or redirect the report to the relevant vendor or upstream project.
